content.full_text:
State of California                                   2 2 3 f      Pete Wilson, Governor

CALIFORNIA HORSE RACING BOARD

Henry Chavez, Chairman                           1010 Hurley Way, Suite 190
William M. Lansdale, Vice-Chairman               Sacramento, California 95825
Leslie N. Liscom, Member                         (916) 920-7178
Rosemary P. Ferraro, Member                      FAX (916) 920-7658
Ralph M. Scurfield, Member
Donald Valpredo, Member
Stefan L. Manolakas, Member
Dennis Hutcheson, Executive Secretary            December 23, 1991


California Fair Political Practices Commission
Attn: Jeanette E. Turville
428 J Street, Suite 800
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA    95804-0807


Re:  Conflict of Interest, Regulation 2000
     Racing Officials and Senior Special Investigators


Dear Ms. Turville:

Per your telephone conversation of 12-11-91 with Linda Hayes of my
staff, I am writing to you for clarification and determination of
two unresolved issues regarding the amendment of Conflict of
Interest Regulation No. 2000.

ISSUE NO. 1:

Are Racing Officials public officials as defined in Commission
Regulation No. 18750 or are they private employees of horse racing
associations not subject to conflict of interest rules?

Background:

As Linda informed you, the racing officials listed in our
Regulation No. 2000 are licensees of the Board and employees of the
horse racing association conducting the race.  This raised the
question as to whether or not they are subject to conflict of
interest standards defined in Commission Regulation No. 18750.
These officials, though not employees of the Board, on occasion may
participate in the decision making process of a steward who is a
contract employee of the Board.

ISSUE NO. 2:

Can the Board delete Senior Special Investigators from Regulation
No. 2000?

Background:

Senior Special Investigators conduct field investigations to detect
or verify suspected violations of the Board's laws, rules or
regulations. They are fact finders and report their findings in
writing to the Board of Stewards or to their Supervising
Investigator. Based on the facts contained in the investigator's
report, decisions are made by either a Steward or a Supervising
Investigator. It is our position that they should be eliminated
from this regulation because they are not involved in making
decisions nor do they recommend action to the Board's decision
makers. Rather, it is their supervisor or a Board appointed
Steward who decides which course of action to pursue based on the
investigative report.

I have enclosed copies of the duty statements for Racing Officials
and Senior Special Investigators. If you have any questions or
wish to discuss this matter further, please feel free to contact
Linda Hayes at 920-7664. Thank you for your assistance.

Sincerely,

[signature]

Earlene McCabe, Manager
Policy and Regulation Unit

sections.facts:
As Linda informed you, the racing officials listed in our Regulation No. 2000 are licensees of the Board and employees of the horse racing association conducting the race. This raised the question as to whether or not they are subject to conflict of interest standards defined in Commission Regulation No. 18750. These officials, though not employees of the Board, on occasion may participate in the decision making process of a steward who is a contract employee of the Board.

ISSUE NO. 2:

Can the Board delete Senior Special Investigators from Regulation No. 2000?

Background:

Senior Special Investigators conduct field investigations to detect or verify suspected violations of the Board's laws, rules or regulations. They are fact finders and report their findings in writing to the Board of Stewards or to their Supervising Investigator. Based on the facts contained in the investigator's report, decisions are made by either a Steward or a Supervising Investigator. It is our opinion that they should be eliminated from this regulation because they are not involved in making decisions nor do they recommend action to the Board's decision makers. Rather, it is their supervisor or a Board appointed Steward who decides which course of action to pursue based on the investigative report.

I have enclosed copies of the duty statements for Racing Officials and Senior Special Investigators. If you have any questions or wish to discuss this matter further, please feel free to contact Linda Hayes at 920-7664. Thank you for your assistance.
